title: alnus glutipes
intro: Alnus glutipes, also known as the Japanese alder or black alder, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, specifically in Japan and parts of China and Korea. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Alnus glutipes include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate cough, and soothe sore throats, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds and flu. Alnus glutipes can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine as a dried root or bark extract.
